description_long: This is a vintage-style gramophone record player featuring a large, flared brass horn mounted on a polished wooden base. The base is rectangular with beveled edges and houses a circular turntable platter, which is gray with a blue center. A metallic tonearm with a small stylus is positioned over the platter, and a hand-crank is attached to the front side of the base, indicating manual operation. The brass horn is highly reflective and slightly tarnished, giving it an antique appearance. The overall design is ornate and classic, reminiscent of early 20th-century phonographs, with visible mechanical components and a sturdy, decorative build.
description: A vintage gramophone record player with a large brass horn, polished wooden base, and a hand-crank mechanism. The design is ornate and classic, featuring a gray and blue turntable platter and metallic tonearm.
description_view_0: Front view showing the brass horn, hand-crank, and the tonearm resting on the gray and blue platter atop the wooden base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full width of the horn, the hand-crank extending to the left, and the platter's circular shape.
description_view_2: Rear angled view displaying the curve of the horn, the back of the wooden base, and the tonearm's position over the platter.
description_view_3: Direct view into the mouth of the brass horn, emphasizing its flared, reflective surface and the top of the wooden base.
attribute_turntable_platter: The turntable platter is present, circular, and occupies a central position on the wooden base. It is gray with a blue center, appears to be made of metal or plastic, and has a smooth, matte finish. No visible grooves or intricate patterns are present, but the color contrast is distinct.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, metallic, and slender, extending from the right side of the base over the platter. It is silver in color with a glossy finish and appears to be resting on the platter. The tonearm has a visible joint near its base for pivoting.
attribute_stylus: The stylus is visible as a small, pointed component attached to the end of the tonearm, likely made of metal or plastic. It is positioned over the platter and appears as a separate, distinct part.
